book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XVIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: If any one, under pretence of asceticism, shall fast on Sunday, let him be anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Whoso fasts on the Lord’s day or on the Sabbath let him be anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Eustathius appointed the Lord’s day as a fast, whereas, because Christ rose from the grave and delivered human nature from sin on that day, we should spend it in offering joyous thanks to God.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: But fasting carries with it the idea of grief and sorrow.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: For this reason those who fast on Sunday are subjected to the punishment of anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: By many canons we are warned against fasting or grieving on the festal and joyous Lord’s day, in remembrance of the resurrection of the Lord; but that we should celebrate it and offer thanks to God, that we be raised from the fall of sin.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: But this canon smites the Eustathians with anathema because they taught that the Lord’s days should be fasted.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Canon LXIV. of the Apostolic Canons cuts off such of the laity as shall so fast, and deposes such of the clergy.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: See also Canon LV. of the Council in Trullo.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars I., Dist. xxx., c. vij.
